question_text: Which of the following statements is/are TRUE for a group G?

options.A: If for all x, y ∈ G, (xy)2 = x2y2, then G is commutative.
options.B: If for all x ∈ G, x2 = 1, then G is commutative. Here, 1 is the identity element of G.
options.C: If the order of G is 2, then G is commutative.
options.D: If G is commutative, then a subgroup of G need not be commutative.
